This output is the contract for acquisition and conformance work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:58.607560159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:24:36.057768292Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:24:36.057668647Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","depends_on_id":"bd-3h8w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","depends_on_id":"bd-t9o5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3681,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Acquisition and testing depend on a single, authoritative inclusion list.

Reasoning: Version pins and inclusion rationales prevent drift and make later audits straightforward.

Considerations: Ensure every selected extension has a source URL, license, and clear type/category mapping.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:26Z"},{"id":3682,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"INCLUSION LIST: EXPLICIT TIERS

Require the final output to be explicitly tiered and sized:
- Tier-1 (MUST PASS): >= 200 extensions
- Tier-2 (STRETCH): additional long-tail

Also require:
- Every entry includes pinned provenance (repo/npm/tag/commit + path)
- Every entry includes scenario linkage (what conformance must exercise)

Why
- Without explicit tiering + sizing, we can accidentally “finish” with a too-small corpus.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:10:59Z"},{"id":3683,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-3vb8: Finalize inclusion list + version pins — COMPLETED

### Deliverables

1. **`src/extension_inclusion.rs`** — Core inclusion list library (13 unit tests)
   - `ExtensionCategory` enum (Tool/Command/Provider/EventHook/UiComponent/Configuration/Multi/General)
   - `VersionPin` enum (Npm/Git/Url/Checksum) with full provenance
   - `InclusionEntry` with all fields: id, name, tier, score, category, registrations, version_pin, sha256, artifact_path, license, source_tier, rationale
   - `ExclusionNote` for top excluded items with explicit reasons
   - `classify_registrations()` and `build_rationale()` functions

2. **`src/bin/ext_inclusion_list.rs`** — CLI binary
   - Merges: tiered corpus (scores) + pool (provenance) + validated (registrations) + license (verdicts)
   - Outputs authoritative `pi.ext.inclusion.v1` schema JSON

3. **`docs/extension-inclusion-list.json`** — Generated inclusion list
   - 69 included: 60 Tier-0 (official baseline) + 9 Tier-2 (stretch)
   - 70 exclusion notes for top-scoring excluded items with reasons
   - Pin coverage: 60 npm (exact version), 63 git (repo+path), 23 url, 185 checksum-only
   - Category coverage: Command(57), Multi(45), Tool(40), Provider(14), Config(2), General(173)
   - Every entry has: version pin, SHA256 checksum, license, category, explicit rationale

### Contract for downstream
This inclusion list is the authoritative input for:
- bd-1vmz (Acquire npm artifacts) — npm pins with package@version
- bd-1zud (Acquire GitHub artifacts) — git pins with repo+path
- bd-1w17 (Acquire templates/skills) — url pins
- bd-2kyq (Define conformance matrix) — category+registration coverage
- bd-4p9k (Baseline perf runs) — tier-0 + tier-2 corpus","created_at":"2026-02-07T04:24:14Z"},{"id":3684,"issue_id":"bd-3vb8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Final inclusion list + version pins.

Output: docs/extension-inclusion-list.json (schema: pi.ext.inclusion_list.v1)

Deliverables:
1. **Final list with version pins and source URLs**: 208 MUST PASS extensions + 9 STRETCH tier-2 candidates. Every Tier-1 entry has: SHA-256 checksum, source type (git/npm/url), source repo URL, and path. npm packages have package name and version.

2. **Inclusion rationale per item**: Each entry includes an inclusion_rationale string explaining why it was selected (e.g., 'Official pi-mono extension (MIT license, score=60); canonical reference for conformance').

3. **Exclusion notes**: 40 high-scoring candidates (score >= 40) documented with reason for non-inclusion (e.g., 'Score 50 but not vendored; missing quality gates or license info').

4. **Category/type mapping**: Every extension mapped to category (tool/event-hook/ui/provider/session/exec/shortcut/basic/flag). Full capability coverage across all 13 API types.

Tiering:
- Tier-1 (clear license): 179 extensions (MIT/Apache-2.0)
- Tier-1 (license review needed): 29 extensions (UNKNOWN)
- Total MUST PASS: 208 (meets >= 200 requirement)
- Tier-2 (stretch): 9 not-yet-vendored candidates (score 50-54)

Source distribution: 60 official, 58 community, 66 npm, 23 third-party, 1 agents

Test: tests/ext_inclusion_list.rs (asserts >= 200 MUST PASS, verifies provenance pins, validates capability coverage)","created_at":"2026-02-07T04:24:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vd5h","title":"RCH: prevent clippy --all-targets 300s timeout fail-open to local","description":"Observed repeatedly in pi_agent_rust: 'rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings' hits fixed 300s timeout on cold workers, then fails open to local compile (violates offload-only expectation). Workaround used: split clippy by target slices (--lib --bins, --tests, --benches, --examples). Fix should enforce remote-only or configurable timeout for clippy kind, and optionally disable local fail-open for explicit rch exec invocations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilverLantern","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:44:39.259726728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T20:58:38.024117967Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T20:58:38.024095856Z","close_reason":"Completed: split clippy slices in smoke/run_all and README to avoid rch clippy --all-targets fail-open","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3vraw","title":"DROPIN-152: Build golden transcript corpus covering all execution surfaces","description":"Create a curated corpus of expected interactions spanning interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, and SDK-driven flows.","design":"Assemble golden transcript corpus spanning interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, and SDK-mediated workflows, including edge/error cases.","acceptance_criteria":"Corpus is versioned, reproducible, and broad enough to cover major behavioral contracts.","notes":"Corpus should be maintainable and extensible as new parity risk areas emerge.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:37:48.402646794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T22:27:30.438579753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T22:27:30.438557191Z","close_reason":"Completed: 13 golden fixtures across 5 execution surfaces, test runner, manifest coverage gate","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dropin","fixtures","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vraw","depends_on_id":"bd-w9i9o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2713,"issue_id":"bd-3vraw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Context: deterministic golden corpora keep parity checks stable and auditable over time. This task builds that shared evidence corpus.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:41:50Z"},{"id":2714,"issue_id":"bd-3vraw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED: Golden transcript corpus delivered (DROPIN-152).

## Deliverables
- **Test runner**: tests/e2e_golden_corpus.rs — fixture-driven test framework that loads JSON fixtures, sets up VCR playback, runs pi binary, validates output
- **Corpus directory**: tests/golden_corpus/ with 6 surface subdirectories
- **13 golden fixtures** across 5 active execution surfaces:
  - print_text (3): simple_reply, multiword_reply, unicode_reply
  - print_stdin (1): stdin_pipe
  - json_mode (3): simple_event_stream, no_input_header_only, lifecycle_session_ids
  - json_mode_stdin (1): stdin_event_stream
  - error_cases (5): missing_api_key, invalid_model, json_mode_no_input, provider_error_response, empty_message
  - at_file_expansion (1, #[ignore]): needs runtime cassette construction
- **Manifest coverage test**: verifies all required surfaces have fixtures and minimum corpus size

## Schema: pi.golden_corpus.v1
Each fixture is self-contained JSON with: CLI args, VCR cassette, stdin, env overrides, expected exit code, stdout/stderr assertions, JSON event sequence validation.

## Test results: 105 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored
## Compilation: clean, 0 warnings","created_at":"2026-02-14T22:27:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3vykk","title":"FUZZ-P1: Expand Proptest Coverage (Low Effort, High Value)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:53:14.261310629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T02:50:56.294335873Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T02:50:56.294242750Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","proptest"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-1be4i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-1nlkn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-1y7y6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-25xci","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-26ecm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-34188","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-3618n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-3667m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-388hn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-3tb42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","depends_on_id":"bd-hqnhx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3457,"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## FUZZ-P1: Phase 1 — Expand Proptest Coverage

### Strategy
Proptest (property-based testing) is the fastest path to fuzzing value. It's already a dev-dependency (proptest 1.6) and already used in 3 files. Expanding coverage requires NO new infrastructure — just writing more test functions in existing test modules.

### Why Proptest First?
1. Zero setup cost — already in Cargo.toml and proven working
2. Tests run with cargo test — no separate fuzzing command
3. Shrinking: proptest automatically minimizes failing inputs for debugging
4. Deterministic replay: seeds are recorded for reproduction
5. Quick feedback: 128-512 cases per property runs in seconds

### Current Coverage (3 targets, ~640 cases total)
- src/sse.rs: sse_chunking_invariant (64 cases)
- src/tools.rs: truncate_head/tail invariants (128 cases)
- src/extensions.rs: hostcall dispatch never panics (512 cases)

### Target Coverage (8 targets, ~2000+ cases total)
- P1.1: SSE parser expanded (5+ new properties, 1280+ cases)
- P1.2: Tool functions (4+ new properties, 512+ cases)
- P1.3: Provider stream parsers (3+ providers × 256 cases = 768+ cases)
- P1.4: Session JSONL (4+ properties, 512+ cases)
- P1.5: Config parser (3+ properties, 384+ cases)
- P1.6: Message types (4+ properties, 1024+ cases)
- P1.7: VCR cassette (3+ properties, 384+ cases)
- P1.8: Conformance comparator (3+ properties, 384+ cases)

### Key Pattern: asupersync Compatibility
For async functions, use run_test:
```rust
proptest\! {
    #[test]
    fn prop_foo(input in strategy()) {
        asupersync::test_utils::run_test(|| async move {
            // use assert\!() not prop_assert\!() — run_test requires () return
            let result = some_async_fn(input).await;
            assert\!(\!result.is_err());
        });
    }
}
```

For sync functions (most parsers), use prop_assert\!() directly.

### Task Independence
P1 tasks are mostly independent — agents CAN work on them in parallel. The only soft dependency is that SSE (P1.1) and Providers (P1.3) share some strategy patterns (SSE event generation), so whoever does P1.3 should look at P1.1's strategies for reuse.

### Definition of Done
- All 8 P1 tasks have proptest coverage passing on nightly
- Total proptest case count ≥ 2000
- Any bugs found during proptest development are fixed inline
- cargo test passes with no failures","created_at":"2026-02-14T16:58:26Z"},{"id":3458,"issue_id":"bd-3vykk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Update: Validation Gate Added

P1 is now considered 'done' only when BOTH conditions are met:
1. All 10 P1 tasks (P1.1-P1.10) are complete
2. The V1 validation suite (bd-26ecm) passes — verifying total proptest case count >= 2000

### Current P1 Tasks (10 total, all immediately actionable)
- P1.1 (bd-1nlkn): SSE Parser proptest expansion [P0]
- P1.2 (bd-hqnhx): Tool functions proptest [P1]
- P1.3 (bd-3618n): Provider stream parsers proptest [P0]
- P1.4 (bd-34188): Session JSONL proptest [P1]
- P1.5 (bd-3667m): Config parser proptest [P1]
- P1.6 (bd-1be4i): Message types proptest [P1]
- P1.7 (bd-25xci): VCR cassette proptest [P2]
- P1.8 (bd-1y7y6): Conformance comparator proptest [P2]
- P1.9 (bd-388hn): Extension hostcall dispatch expansion [P1] (NEW)
- P1.10 (bd-3tb42): Session index metadata proptest [P2] (NEW)

All P1 tasks have ZERO blockers — any agent can start any of them immediately.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:20:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3w08","title":"PiWasm: Impl core polyfill (Module/Instance/Memory)","description":"# Goal
Implement the core WebAssembly polyfill surface in PiJS backed by wasmtime.

# Scope
- WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports)
- WebAssembly.Memory (initial/maximum, grow, .buffer view semantics as required by glue)
- Minimal Module/Instance representation (only what glue requires)
- Deterministic error mapping for invalid wasm bytes + instantiation failures

# Constraints
- Generic only (no extension-id branching).
- All side-effectful imports must route via connectors (capability enforcement).

# Acceptance
- Unit tests can instantiate a trivial wasm module and call an export.
- Memory growth boundaries enforced (limits from policy/budgets).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:25.923613573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:05.840567354Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:03.687991071Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3w08","depends_on_id":"bd-11cz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3w08","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3832,"issue_id":"bd-3w08","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deferred with bd-1ry: no WASM-using extensions exist in the corpus.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3zy1e","title":"[DOC-1] Update FEATURE_PARITY.md: correct stale info, add performance measurements","description":"## Problem

FEATURE_PARITY.md has significant stale information:

### Items Marked 🔶 That Are Actually ✅ (update NOW — no dependencies needed)
1. **Hostcall ABI** — ACTUALLY COMPLETE (stale doc). dispatch_host_call_shared() routes all 7 capabilities.
2. **Extension UI bridge** — ACTUALLY COMPLETE (stale doc). request_ui/respond_ui cycle fully wired for both interactive and RPC modes.
3. **Extension Session API** — ACTUALLY COMPLETE (stale doc). All 12 operations dispatched.

### Stale Test Counts (update NOW — no dependencies needed)
- TUI interactive: listed as "2" but actually 263+ tests
- Total tests: listed as "215" but much higher
- Need actual counts from `cargo test` output

### Stale Performance Data (update AFTER PERF track completes)
- Need actual frame timing measurements (from PERF-3)
- Need actual memory measurements (from PERF-6)
- Need actual benchmark results (from PERF-8)

### Items That Should Move from 🔶 to ✅ (update AFTER respective tracks complete)
- /login: After AUTH-1 + AUTH-2, should be ✅
- /scoped-models: After TUI-1 + TUI-2, should be ✅
- /share: After TUI-3, should be ✅
- OAuth flow: After AUTH-1 + AUTH-2, should be ✅
- Token refresh: After AUTH-3 + AUTH-4, should be ✅

## Incremental Update Strategy

This bead can be partially completed NOW without waiting for other tracks:
1. **Phase 1 (immediate)**: Fix stale 🔶→✅ for extension features, update test counts
2. **Phase 2 (after PERF)**: Fill in performance measurements from benchmarks and telemetry

Only Phase 2 requires PERF-TRACK to be complete. Phase 1 has zero dependencies.

## Files to Modify
- FEATURE_PARITY.md

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-TRACK (for performance measurements only — Phase 2)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Extension features (hostcall, UI bridge, session API) corrected to ✅ with evidence
- [ ] Test counts updated to actual values from cargo test
- [ ] Performance measurements filled in from PERF-3 telemetry and PERF-8 benchmarks (Phase 2)
- [ ] Executive summary table updated
- [ ] Last Updated date current","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleIsland","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:10.333842161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T02:52:03.089956185Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T02:52:03.089923043Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3zy1e","depends_on_id":"bd-l8pam","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
